main.description: A rumor a player SPREADS — written to the PLAYER'S OWN repo (user-owned, $0 to us). This is a WRITE-ONLY OUTBOX from the client's side: the game never reads its own rumors back. The engine (hoopy) tails this collection off the firehose and MAY choose to respond by writing a com.minomobi.hoop.story.verdict (a retcon/notice aimed at this player or others) or a com.minomobi.hoop.story.content (new shared material) — both of which the client already consumes. So a rumor is an append-only event, NOT a field on the save aggregate: rkey = a TID (time-ordered, never rewritten), the opposite of story.save's latest-wins snapshot. The save holds at most a cursor, never rumor bodies. Cross-player propagation is the engine's job (and a future PvP surface); the client only ever writes here. Retained by reference — a rumor names its subject (a content_item id), it does not copy the content.

world.description: World key (ship seed) this rumor pertains to.

kind.description: OPTIONAL discriminator for what KIND of rumor this is, so the engine can route it. 'sighting' (the spread-word default — a content_item was met), 'divination' (a worship-room oracle omen — yijing/geomancy — the entropic-omen signal), 'inkblot' (a govern-room seal: a Rorschach archetype + the player's colour). Absent ⇒ treat as 'sighting'.

text.description: The rumor body — what is being spread, in words. For divination it is the omen; for an inkblot it names the figure + the player's colour.

seed.description: OPTIONAL reproducibility seed: for kind 'divination'/'inkblot', re-running the same deterministic cast/blot-generation from this seed reproduces the reading exactly.

profileJson.description: OPTIONAL JSON-encoded ARCHETYPE PROFILE for kind 'divination' (hexagram/figure + trigrams/planet/judgment) or 'inkblot' (portrait title/blurb/axes + objective traits). Carried as a string (like story.save's stateJson) so the structured shape can evolve without a lexicon bump.

color.description: OPTIONAL free-text 'colour' the player adds to an inkblot seal — what they saw in it. Their words, alongside the blot's measured archetype.

subject.description: OPTIONAL reference: the content_item id (or at-uri) this rumor is ABOUT. The engine resolves it against the pool. Retained by reference — the content itself is not copied here.

subjectType.description: The content type of the subject (npc / creature / item / lore_fragment / plot_beat / rumor).

subjectName.description: Display name of the subject at spread time (a convenience label; the binding is `subject`).

tier.description: Revelation tier of the subject when the rumor was spread (1..5, the Tabard range).
